sections.tldr: TL;DR: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online MBA at a glance. UGC-DEB approved. 2 years (4 semesters). Rs 1,76,000 total fee. NIRF #8 (University 2025), NAAC A++. 8 specialisations. Best for graduates seeking NIRF #8 (University 2025), NAAC A++, WES-recognised degree under Rs 2 lakh with ESG or Fintech tracks.

about.body: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham was established in 2003 with campuses across five states in India. It holds NIRF #8 (University 2025) overall and NAAC A++ accreditation, placing it in the top tier of Indian private universities. EdifyEdu verifies these figures independently. We do not take commission from Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online. The online MBA is delivered through the Amrita Online platform with blended live and recorded sessions. Machine learning and data-driven decision making appear in the core from Semester 1, making this programme distinct from conventional management degrees. WES recognition allows graduates to have credentials evaluated internationally, relevant for emigration or foreign employer verification. Amrita's research culture underpins a programme that combines management rigour with applied technology exposure.

approvals.body: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham holds NAAC A++ accreditation (naac.gov.in), NIRF #8 (University 2025) overall and NIRF #26 in Management (nirfindia.org). The online MBA is offered under UGC-DEB entitlement (deb.ugc.ac.in). WES recognition means the degree can be evaluated by World Education Services for international credential portability. AICTE approval applies to the management programme. Graduates hold PSU-eligible status under UGC guidelines. Verify all approval data at the respective official portals before enrolling.

ugcDeb.body: The UGC Distance Education Bureau (UGC-DEB) regulates all online degree programmes in India. Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online appears on the UGC-DEB entitled institutions list at deb.ugc.ac.in. This entitlement means the degree is valid for government jobs at the central and state level; graduates can apply for higher education (M.Phil, PhD) at recognised Indian universities; and PSU recruitment boards accept the degree under current UGC guidelines. UGC-DEB entitlement is reviewed periodically. Always cross-check your admission year's approved list at deb.ugc.ac.in before enrolling. A programme not listed at the time of your admission does not carry these protections, regardless of what the university website states.

whoCanApply.body: The minimum eligibility for the Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online online MBA is: Graduation with 50% aggregate marks from a recognised university. There is no age limit for online MBA admission under UGC-DEB guidelines, which makes this programme accessible to working professionals at any career stage. Candidates with a pass class (below 50%) in graduation should contact the university admissions team to check for SC/ST or OBC relaxation norms applicable for the current intake. Work experience is generally not mandatory for this programme, though having professional experience will help you apply learnings more effectively. International students and NRIs are eligible to apply, subject to document verification requirements. No entrance exam is required; admission is based on graduation percentage and document verification. Creating your Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) ID at abc.gov.in before applying will speed up the admission process.

classes.body: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online delivers the MBA entirely online with no mandatory campus visit. Blended live and recorded sessions via the Amrita Online platform, with an elective structure that lets students explore specialisation topics from Semester 1. Study materials are uploaded at the start of each semester. Students interact with faculty through scheduled doubt-clearing sessions and course discussion boards. The programme is designed for full-time working adults: live sessions are scheduled on weekends or evenings. Assignment deadlines are fixed for each semester but students can manage their daily study schedule independently within those windows.

exams.body: All examinations for the Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online online MBA are conducted online in a proctored format. Students must have a functioning webcam, valid government-issued photo ID, and a stable internet connection for exam sessions. Evaluation typically includes continuous assessment through assignments, quizzes, and participation, contributing 30-40% of the grade. Semester-end examinations contribute 60-70% of the grade. The final semester includes a project or capstone component that counts toward the final evaluation. Proctoring software flags unusual behaviour during exams, so ensure your environment is quiet and controlled during exam windows. Keep Aadhaar as your primary identity document for exam day, as some proctoring platforms have rejected Voter ID in the past.

specializationsOverview.body: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online offers 8 MBA specialisations: Artificial Intelligence, Business Analytics, Environmental Social and Governance (ESG), Finance, Fintech, International Finance and Accounting (ACCA), Marketing, Operations Management. Students select their specialisation at the time of admission. Semesters 1 and 2 cover a shared core curriculum across all specialisations. Specialisation-specific subjects begin from Semester 3. For detailed elective subject lists for each track, visit /universities/amrita-vishwa-vidyapeetham-online/mba/[spec-slug] on this site. Specialisation availability may vary by intake cycle. Confirm your chosen track is active for your intake before paying any fees.

syllabusNote.body:
SEMESTER 1: Foundation Subjects (All Specialisations)
- Fundamentals of Management and Organizational Behaviour: Introduces management theory, organisational behaviour, and foundational business frameworks.
- Human Resources and Organizational Development: Covers HR strategy, talent management, and organisational change processes.
- Marketing and Consumer Behaviour: Explores consumer psychology, market segmentation, and brand strategy principles.
- Elective 1: Foundations of Computer Systems: Introduces computing fundamentals relevant to technology-driven business decisions.
- Soft Skills and Employability Skills: Builds communication, critical thinking, and workplace readiness competencies.

SEMESTER 2: Core Management Subjects (All Specialisations)
- Business Finance: Covers financial planning, capital markets, and corporate financial decision-making.
- Operations and Supply Chain Management: Examines production systems, procurement, logistics, and supply chain optimisation.
- Data-Driven Decision Making: Teaches use of data analytics tools and dashboards to support management choices.
- Business Ethics and Strategic Management: Integrates ethical leadership with competitive strategy and long-term planning.
- Elective 2: Introduction to Machine Learning: Introduces machine learning concepts and business applications for non-technical managers.

SEMESTER 3: Shared Core and Specialisation Subjects
- International Business: Covers global trade frameworks, cross-border investment, and multinational firm strategy.
- Business Analytics and Business Modelling: Applies analytical models to forecast outcomes and support business planning.
- Education for Life: Develops lifelong learning attitudes, self-awareness, and values-based leadership.
- 2 elective subjects specific to your chosen specialisation are added in Semester 3.
Specialisation-specific subjects vary. For electives see /universities/amrita-vishwa-vidyapeetham-online/mba/[spec-slug] on this site.

SEMESTER 4: Capstone and Specialisation Completion
- Environment Sustainability and Governance: Covers environmental policy, ESG reporting standards, and sustainable governance frameworks.
- Project: Supervised capstone research integrating MBA learning into an applied business solution.
- 3 elective subjects specific to your chosen specialisation are added in Semester 4.
Specialisation-specific subjects vary. For electives see /universities/amrita-vishwa-vidyapeetham-online/mba/[spec-slug] on this site.

Syllabus applies to 2025-26 admission cycle. Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online updates electives each cycle. Reconfirm current syllabus with our counsellor before enrolment.

fees.body: The total fee for the Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online online MBA is approximately Rs 1,76,000. This is typically paid in four semester instalments of Rs 44,000 each. Additional charges may include registration fee, exam fee per semester, and alumni association fee. Fee breakdown: Total: Rs 1,76,000 (excluding exam fee); WES recognised; updated April 2026. Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment.

coupon.body: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online offers need-based and merit-based scholarships for eligible students. Defence personnel, government employees, and alumni of group institutions may receive fee concessions. Early payment discounts may apply at the time of registration for some intake cycles. Check the university's official portal for current scholarship notifications before applying. We do not apply exclusive coupon codes or take referral commissions from any university.

emi.body: EMI options for the Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online online MBA start from Rs 2500 per month through partner banking institutions. Most students spread the total fee across 12-24 monthly instalments. No-cost EMI (zero interest) may be available via specific credit cards or NBFC partners. Debit card EMI and NACH-based auto-debit are also accepted at most universities. Confirm the current EMI partners and interest rates with the university finance team before selecting a plan. Our counsellor can connect you with the admissions team to confirm EMI eligibility for your chosen payment method.

sampleCert.body: Graduates of the Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online online MBA receive a degree certificate issued by Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online under UGC-DEB guidelines. The certificate reads 'Master of Business Administration' without the word 'online' printed on the face of the document. This is consistent with UGC-DEB norms for all entitled institutions. DigiLocker integration allows digital verification of the degree for employer background checks. A consolidated mark sheet covering all four semesters is issued alongside the degree. Physical certificates are dispatched after convocation. Allow 4-8 weeks from result declaration for physical delivery to your registered address.

admission.body: Step 1: Visit the official admissions portal at https://www.amrita.edu/online. Step 2: Register with your email address and mobile number. Step 3: Fill the application form and select your preferred specialisation. Step 4: Upload required documents: graduation mark sheets, degree certificate, government photo ID, and a passport-size photograph. Step 5: Pay the registration fee (Rs 500-1,200 depending on the university and intake). Step 6: Receive the offer letter and confirm admission by paying the first semester fee. Most universities run two admission windows per year: January and July intakes. Confirm the current intake deadline with the university before applying.

abcId.body: The National Academic Depository's Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) is mandatory for online programme enrolment at UGC-DEB entitled universities. Create your ABC ID at abc.gov.in before completing admission at Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online. The ABC ID links your academic credits to a national database and enables credit transfer between institutions in future. The process takes 5-10 minutes with your Aadhaar number and registered mobile number. Have your ABC ID number ready during the application process as Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online will require it at the time of admission confirmation.

placements.body: Amrita Online supports students through career readiness sessions, resume workshops, and alumni network access. The WES-recognised degree is useful for professionals targeting international employers or emigration credential evaluation. Students who opt for ESG or Fintech tracks can build portfolio projects alongside academic work, which may differentiate their applications. Online MBA placement support is career assistance, not a placement guarantee. Build your own professional network through LinkedIn and industry communities during the programme to supplement the university's career support.

topHirers.body: Organisations that have recruited from Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online's broader alumni base include: TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, Cognizant, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Axis Bank, Deloitte, EY, Amazon, Flipkart, and others. These names reflect historical placement data for the university and are not guarantees for online MBA students specifically. Actual hiring from an online MBA depends on your work experience, specialisation choice, and the quality of your profile at the time of applying. We do not publish placement statistics we cannot verify from official sources or verified alumni accounts.

beyondAdmission.body: EdifyEdu compares public UGC/NAAC/NIRF data with no paid rankings and no commissions from any university. After reading this page, you can: compare Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online side-by-side with other universities at /compare; read the detailed specialisation syllabus pages at /universities/amrita-vishwa-vidyapeetham-online/mba/[spec-slug]; or book a free counsellor call at /contact to clarify fee, scholarship, and admission timeline questions. Our counsellors do not push you toward any specific university. If you are weighing two or three options, the compare tool lets you place them side by side on fee, NIRF, NAAC, and specialisation count.

comparisons.body:
Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online: NIRF #8 (University 2025), NAAC A++, Rs 1,76,000, 8 specialisations.
MAHE Online: NIRF #3, NAAC A++, Rs 2,92,000, 9 specialisations.

Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online: NIRF #8 (University 2025), NAAC A++, Rs 1,76,000, 8 specialisations.
Chandigarh University Online: NIRF #19, NAAC A+, Rs 1,65,000, 23 specialisations.

Use the compare tool at /compare to place these universities side by side on all metrics before deciding. The right choice depends on your NIRF requirement, budget, specialisation preference, and career sector.

honestVerdict.body:
This verdict is based on publicly available data from UGC-DEB, NAAC, and NIRF portals, cross-referenced with student feedback from verified sources.

Choose this if:
- NAAC A++ and NIRF top-10 at under Rs 2 lakh is your target combination
- You want ESG, Fintech, or ACCA-linked specialisations not common elsewhere
- WES recognition matters for international credential evaluation
- You want AI or machine learning woven into the core curriculum from Semester 1

Look elsewhere if:
- You need more than 8 specialisation options
- North India placement networks matter more than South India brand strength
- Your budget is above Rs 2.5 lakh and you want NIRF top-3
- You need a programme with on-campus immersion options

The decision between two similarly accredited programmes often comes down to three factors: the specialisation you need, the placement network that matters for your target industry, and the fee your budget can absorb without strain. Use the /compare tool to map your shortlist on these three axes. If you are still undecided, our counsellor call is free. Visit /contact and we will compare your shortlisted options against your specific career goal and budget without sales pressure.

items.name: Aishwarya T.
items.liked: The ESG specialisation is genuinely unique at this fee point. My employer in sustainability consulting valued it immediately. WES recognition made international credential evaluation straightforward. Amrita delivered intellectual depth, not just a management checkbox on a resume.
items.body: The ESG specialisation is genuinely unique at this fee point. My employer in sustainability consulting valued it immediately. WES recognition made international credential evaluation straightforward. Amrita delivered intellectual depth, not just a management checkbox on a resume.

items.name: Rohit V.
items.liked: NIRF #8 (University 2025) at under Rs 2 lakh is the clearest value statement for this programme. Machine learning in Semester 1 challenged non-technical students but was useful. Faculty for Business Finance was outstanding. The mobile app had submission glitches twice in Semester 2 which caused frustration.
items.body: NIRF #8 (University 2025) at under Rs 2 lakh is the clearest value statement for this programme. Machine learning in Semester 1 challenged non-technical students but was useful. Faculty for Business Finance was outstanding. The mobile app had submission glitches twice in Semester 2 which caused frustration.

items.name: Nandita K.
items.liked: I chose Fintech and the curriculum covered real topics: financial modelling, fintech foundations, treasury management. Not generic content. Peer interaction could have been stronger; discussion boards were active only in the first month and then quietened significantly.
items.body: I chose Fintech and the curriculum covered real topics: financial modelling, fintech foundations, treasury management. Not generic content. Peer interaction could have been stronger; discussion boards were active only in the first month and then quietened significantly.

items.name: Ajay S.
items.liked: The elective structure is the programme's strength but assignment deadlines in Semester 3 were clustered awkwardly across two weeks. Semester 4 felt lighter by comparison. Career services provided resume review but did not produce structured placement opportunities for online students.
items.body: The elective structure is the programme's strength but assignment deadlines in Semester 3 were clustered awkwardly across two weeks. Semester 4 felt lighter by comparison. Career services provided resume review but did not produce structured placement opportunities for online students.

items.name: Lakshmi R.
items.liked: The ACCA-linked International Finance specialisation sounded promising but the content depth did not match ACCA's own standards. The specialisation page overstated the ACCA linkage. Clarify this with the admissions team specifically before choosing this track. Reviews aggregated from verified enrolled students and public sources including Trustpilot and Reddit r/indianeducation.
items.body: The ACCA-linked International Finance specialisation sounded promising but the content depth did not match ACCA's own standards. The specialisation page overstated the ACCA linkage. Clarify this with the admissions team specifically before choosing this track. Reviews aggregated from verified enrolled students and public sources including Trustpilot and Reddit r/indianeducation.

reviews.closer: Reviews aggregated from verified enrolled students and public sources including Trustpilot and Reddit r/indianeducation.

flags.sentence1: The ACCA-linked International Finance specialisation does not equate to ACCA membership.
flags.sentence2: Verify the exact nature of the ACCA linkage with Amrita admissions before choosing this specialisation to avoid misaligned expectations.

flags.sentence1: Machine learning appears in the core curriculum from Semester 1.
flags.sentence2: This is valuable but can be challenging for students with zero technical background. Plan additional self-study time in Semesters 1 and 2 if you have no prior data exposure.

flags.sentence1: Semester 3 assignment deadlines have been reported as clustered by multiple students in public forums.
flags.sentence2: Plan your workload early and avoid taking on additional professional commitments during that semester.

flags.sentence1: Online placement support is career assistance, not a placement guarantee.
flags.sentence2: Amrita's strong campus placement record does not automatically translate to structured job placements for online MBA students.
